name: Systemic Intertwining Framework
summary: Cross-system dependency framework documenting how divine logic, Doomsday residue, institutions, logistics, class pressure, and NPC lived history generate present-day conflicts and play surfaces.
design_intent: make every major world system visibly affect at least three other systems
convert abstract lore into locality, faction, route, and NPC consequences
support GM diagnosis of why a conflict happens here, why now, and who profits from stasis

core_dependency_chains.source: pantheon fragment logic and religious interpretation
intermediate_steps: house doctrine, guild ritual, archive ethic, and court ceremony
policy preferences on law, education, trade, burial, and acceptable vice
public expectations about legitimacy, shame, mercy, memory, and ambition
downstream_effects: what kinds of NPC hypocrisy are culturally tolerated
core_dependency_chains.play_surface: A scene with priests, judges, archivists, wardens, or guild brokers should reveal not only procedure but the divine philosophy embedded inside that procedure.

intermediate_steps: world flipping, node storms, magical contamination, sea instability, and memory anxiety
route hardening, storage customs, calendar variance, fortified architecture, and ritual forecasting
scarcity windows, seasonal fear, insurance logic, and regional propaganda
core_dependency_chains.play_surface: A missing person, cargo delay, or local law dispute should be traceable to a Doomsday-shaped material condition somewhere in the chain.

core_dependency_chains.source: road, harbor, bridge, and caravan control
core_dependency_chains.play_surface: Movement should always change the political meaning of the party and not merely their map position.

core_dependency_chains.source: age, species lifespan, locality, class, and witnessed historical transitions
intermediate_steps: education access, debt exposure, grief exposure, service record, and faith contact
moral habits, social caution, ambition ceiling, faction taste, and speech style
downstream_effects: who responds poorly to outsiders or procedure
core_dependency_chains.play_surface: NPCs should answer questions differently not only because of personality but because history trained them to price risk differently.

core_dependency_chains.source: regional values, ritual expectations, and climate/resource realities
downstream_effects: which negotiations can happen privately versus publicly
core_dependency_chains.play_surface: The same act should create different consequences in Shelk, Sheresh, Soreheim, Psanan, and Nomdara.

The Principalities: connect knowledge, contemplation, compassion, fear, and renewal to noble procedure and guild legitimacy
show how road control, book tariffs, treasury capture, prison labor, and afterlife commerce create shared but competing institutional dependency
make status shame and paper-trace exposure as dangerous as open violence
Sheresh Communes: connect magical pollution to art, shelter, exile, mutation fear, and research patronage
show how dome security and beauty culture justify containment authority
make every magical convenience carry contamination management implications
Soreheim Alliance: connect tower engineering, caste duty, scarcity, giant governance, and industrial progress
show how production ideology shapes mercy, food access, labor coercion, and outsider status
tie descent routes and unfinished towers to social rank and strategic fragility
Psanan: connect matriarchal domestic law, blood-vow seriousness, hardness values, monster ecology, and gift economy
show how war customs alter marriage, authority, and household access
make independence and bravery materially costly rather than merely aesthetic
Nomdara: connect diplomacy, barter mobility, coordinated purchase strikes, and route memory
show how soft power and staple control produce external restraint without permanent territorial sovereignty
make every agreement with Nomdara alter local market confidence

gm_application_questions: Which divine or ideological logic legitimizes this institution?
Which Doomsday residue still materially shapes this locality?
Which route or chokepoint makes this conflict economically meaningful?
Which class or age cohort remembers the last version of this problem?
Which faction gains if procedure slows the players?
How would this same problem mutate if moved to another polity?
